Fleet suggests ^LaAka seems to denote here not
the island of Ceylon, but its capital city, because it provides the
Hindu prime meridian.]
Kaldjina, which is also spelt as KalaAjana, is identical with
Kalafijara, according (o Fleet, According to a Puranic verse,
wn: which is recited at Sraddhas,
it is a mountain of some religious importance. We cannot be sure
of its location. It may be a mountain in the south abounding
in Krsnamrgas, a type of spotted dear, whose skins are used for
various religious purposes such as the Upanayana.
Sauriklrnas ; Kern splits this word as Sauri and Kirna and
identifies the former with the Sorae or Colas of Ptolemy.
Tnlikata is identified by Fleet with Talikot in the Bijapur
district. A.M. Shastri feels that it would be more appropriate to
identify it with Talakadu in Mysore, the celebrated capital of the
GaAgas. In my view Talikot is preferable.
Girinagara is the modern Junagadh, which is immortalized by
the Girnar inscription of Rudradaman I.
